name: Skip-Bo
description: Skip-Bo is Mattel's bright sequence-stacking card game — race to deplete your personal stockpile by playing cards onto shared building piles in strict 1-to-12 order. Wild cards bridge gaps, four discard piles let you dump dead-ends, and one well-timed run can empty half your stockpile in a turn. Originally invented by Hazel Bowman in the 1960s and a fixture of family game cabinets ever since.
